name: Honoré Dutrey
abstract: Honoré Dutrey (c. 1894 in New Orleans, Louisiana – July 21, 1935 in Chicago, Illinois) was a Dixieland jazz trombonist, probably best known for his work in King Oliver's Creole Jazz Band.

about_quotes.speaker: Preston Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: I was fortunate enough to, to have two great trombones. This is, I'd say as teachers, it was Honoré Dutré and Rod Palmer.

about_quotes.speaker: Alberta Hunter
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] (Inaudible), Honore Dutrey and -- Baby Dodds >> [INTERVIEWEE] Honoré Dutrey, Bechet -- Bechet --

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Henry
about_quotes.evidence: He held my hand and showed me the second position... I'm going to give you this... come up here twice a week.

about_quotes.speaker: Alberta Hunter
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] (Inaudible), Honore Dutrey and -- Baby Dodds [INTERVIEWEE] Honoré Dutrey, Bechet -- Bechet --

about_quotes.speaker: George P. Fleming
about_quotes.evidence: they had Sam Dutre he played tenor and he was a good clarinet man... and he was the nephew of Honoré Dutre

about_quotes.speaker: Avakian George
about_quotes.evidence: He picked out Honoré Dutrey and John Lindsay on bass, as I recall, and Boyd Atkins.
about_quotes.doc_title: Avakian George — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
